Moringa (Moringa oleifera) is a tropical tree native to India and Pakistan, known for its edible leaves, seeds, and pods, which are rich in nutrients and have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Moringa include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Moringa include isothiocyanates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Moringa is often used to make herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, and oil, which are commonly used to support overall health and well-being.
